Container.Name: speedtest-monitor
Container.Overview:
Self-hosted internet speed monitor. Runs Cloudflare Speed Test on a configurable schedule, stores every measurement in SQLite, and serves a dashboard with live updates over WebSocket. Includes alerts (webhook, ntfy, Discord, Slack, SMTP), authentication (local accounts and OIDC), and role-based access (admin / viewer).
On first boot, set AUTH_SECRET (required) and either set SPEEDTEST_ADMIN_EMAIL + SPEEDTEST_ADMIN_PASSWORD to seed the first admin, or visit /setup to create it from the UI. All other variables are optional.
Data is persisted to /data (mapped to /mnt/user/appdata/speedtest-monitor by default).
Container.Description:
Self-hosted internet speed monitor with a live dashboard, history charts, alerts, and authentication.
Features: - Cloudflare Speed Test (HTTP-only, no browser, no Chromium dependency) - Configurable schedule (1-1440 min), live-editable from the UI - SQLite history with download / upload / latency / jitter / packet loss / bufferbloat - Live updates over WebSocket (path: /ws) - Alerts on degradation and recovery via webhook, ntfy, Discord, Slack, SMTP - Local accounts + optional OIDC SSO (Authelia, Authentik, Keycloak, ...) - admin / viewer roles
Setup notes: - AUTH_SECRET is REQUIRED. Generate with: openssl rand -base64 32 - For the first admin, set SPEEDTEST_ADMIN_EMAIL + SPEEDTEST_ADMIN_PASSWORD (idempotent seed) OR visit /setup on first boot. - If you front this container with a reverse proxy (SWAG, NPM, Caddy, ...), keep AUTH_TRUST_HOST=true and forward the Upgrade header on /ws so the live dashboard works.

Config.Name: WebUI Port
Config.Description: HTTP + WebSocket port served by the app.

Config.Name: App Data
Config.Description: Persistent storage for the SQLite database (speedtest.db).

Config.Name: AUTH_SECRET
Config.Description: REQUIRED. Secret used to sign session cookies. Generate with: openssl rand -base64 32

Config.Name: SPEEDTEST_INTERVAL_MINUTES
Config.Description: Default measurement interval in minutes (1-1440). Can be changed at runtime from /settings without restarting.

Config.Name: SPEEDTEST_ADMIN_EMAIL
Config.Description: First-run admin email (optional). When set together with SPEEDTEST_ADMIN_PASSWORD, the admin is auto-seeded on every boot (idempotent). Leave empty to use the /setup wizard instead.

Config.Name: SPEEDTEST_ADMIN_PASSWORD
Config.Description: First-run admin password (optional, min 10 chars). Used together with SPEEDTEST_ADMIN_EMAIL. Updating this value rehashes the admin password on the next boot.

Config.Name: SPEEDTEST_PUBLIC_URL
Config.Description: Public URL of the dashboard (e.g. https://speedtest.example.com). Used to build deep links in alert emails. Leave empty if unused.

Config.Name: AUTH_TRUST_HOST
Config.Description: Trust the X-Forwarded-* headers. Keep true when running behind a reverse proxy (SWAG, NPM, Caddy, Traefik).

Config.Name: SPEEDTEST_TEST_DURATION_S
Config.Description: Duration of each download/upload phase in seconds. Raise to 20-30 for multi-Gbps links so TCP slow-start does not dominate the sample.

Config.Name: SPEEDTEST_PARALLEL_STREAMS
Config.Description: Parallel HTTP streams per phase. Increase (e.g. 16) on very high-bandwidth links to saturate the pipe.

Config.Name: SPEEDTEST_DB_PATH
Config.Description: Absolute path to the SQLite database file. Must live under the App Data volume.

Config.Name: PORT
Config.Description: Internal HTTP port. Only change if you know what you are doing - must match the WebUI port mapping above.

Config.Name: SPEEDTEST_WEBHOOK_URL
Config.Description: Generic webhook URL for alerts (optional). Leave empty if unused.

Config.Name: SPEEDTEST_WEBHOOK_HEADERS
Config.Description: Optional JSON object of extra headers for the generic webhook (e.g. {"Authorization":"Bearer xxx"}).

Config.Name: SPEEDTEST_NTFY_URL
Config.Description: Full ntfy URL including the topic, e.g. https://ntfy.sh/my-topic. Leave empty if unused.

Config.Name: SPEEDTEST_NTFY_TOKEN
Config.Description: Optional ntfy access token (for protected topics).

Config.Name: SPEEDTEST_DISCORD_WEBHOOK
Config.Description: Discord webhook URL for alerts (optional).

Config.Name: SPEEDTEST_SLACK_WEBHOOK
Config.Description: Slack incoming webhook URL for alerts (optional).

Config.Name: SPEEDTEST_SMTP_HOST
Config.Description: SMTP server hostname for email alerts (optional). Required together with SPEEDTEST_SMTP_FROM and SPEEDTEST_SMTP_TO to enable email.

Config.Name: SPEEDTEST_SMTP_PORT
Config.Description: SMTP port. Defaults to 587 (STARTTLS).

Config.Name: SPEEDTEST_SMTP_SECURE
Config.Description: 'true' for implicit TLS (port 465), 'false' for STARTTLS, leave empty for auto-detect from port.

Config.Name: SPEEDTEST_SMTP_USER
Config.Description: SMTP username (optional, for authenticated SMTP).

Config.Name: SPEEDTEST_SMTP_PASS
Config.Description: SMTP password (optional, for authenticated SMTP).

Config.Name: SPEEDTEST_SMTP_FROM
Config.Description: Sender address for alert emails, e.g. speedtest@example.com.

Config.Name: SPEEDTEST_SMTP_TO
Config.Description: Recipient address for alert emails (single address or comma-separated list).

Config.Name: SPEEDTEST_OIDC_ISSUER
Config.Description: OIDC issuer URL (optional), e.g. https://auth.example.com. Required together with the client id/secret to enable SSO.

Config.Name: SPEEDTEST_OIDC_CLIENT_ID
Config.Description: OIDC client id (optional).

Config.Name: SPEEDTEST_OIDC_CLIENT_SECRET
Config.Description: OIDC client secret (optional).

Config.Name: SPEEDTEST_OIDC_DISPLAY_NAME
Config.Description: Label shown on the OIDC sign-in button.

Config.Name: SPEEDTEST_OIDC_ADMIN_EMAIL
Config.Description: Email of the user to auto-promote to admin on first OIDC sign-in.

Config.Name: SPEEDTEST_OIDC_ALLOW_NEW_USERS
Config.Description: 'true' to auto-create accounts on first OIDC sign-in, 'false' to require admin pre-provisioning.
